text: Patient was placed on right lateral docubitis position and double lumen intubation was performed to achieve unilateral ventilation.
A port incision was placed behind the posterior axillary line at the level of the seventh interspace accommodate, the 5 mm videotaracoscopy.
The borders of the rib lesion were marked with needles and two additional ports were placed 2.5 cm anterior and posterior to the lesion under tracoscopy control.
Then, the bridal pillar under the fifth rib was opened by electrical cautery.
Periostal tissue at proximal and distal end of fifth rib was dissected.
The intercostal vessels were coagulated and cut with the electrical cautery.
The lower margin of fifth rib was blue with a jelly saw.
The ends of the jelly saw were halved with two clumped forceps and the fifth rib was transected.
The upper margin of fifth rib was transected in the same manner.
Antercostal tissue was cut between electrical cautery.
5 cm segment of the rib was resected and a bag was inserted into the trussic cavity through the port for removal of the rib.
One chest tube was inserted via the port wound.
The chest tube was removed on postoper day 2.
The patient was discharged on postoper day 3 without complications.
